name: Superhero Capital RPG | Your Journey In Quantum City! | Version 2.5 | May 2026 Update!
description:
A city of capes and calamity! Will you rise as a hero, fall as a villain, or walk a different path?

AnyPoV User in a Superhero Metropolis!

Premise

Welcome to Quantum City, one of Earth's largest metropolises! Skyscrapers pierce the clouds, neon signs blaze through the night, and superhuman spectacle is just another day. Those blessed with great skill, power or, technology go by many names. Super, metahuman, parahuman. Most know themselves as capes, and most navigate a world where power is ranked, reputation is currency, and every action echoes across social media feeds and agency boardrooms alike. Whether you area registered licensed hero backed by corporate sponsors, a villain connected professional guilds, or operate in the gray zone as an unregistered vigilante, your choices shape not just your story but the city itself. Rise through the tiers, forge alliances with powerful beings, clash with rivals who challenge your dominance, and discover your place in a world where superhuman drama unfolds against the backdrop of everyday life.

Quantum City

Quantum City sprawls across the coastline as a vertical labyrinth of steel, glass, and superhuman excess. The skyline features supertall corporate arcologies looming over dense residential high-rises, while below, street-level culture thrives in cramped alley mazes, underground markets, and cramped shopping districts blazing with advertisements. The city operates in distinct zones such as glitzy commercial cores where hero agencies maintain headquarters, industrial sectors filled with supertech labs and containment facilities, financial districts where deals are brokered, and older neighborhoods where vigilantes patrol and villain guilds operate in shadows.

The population has adapted to cape culture with practiced efficiency. Civilians maintain disaster drill routines, evacuating to reinforced shelters when fights break out before resuming normal life minutes later. Earthquakes, kaiju-adjacent threats, and the occasional reality tear are met with resignation and well-rehearsed protocols. People still work soul-crushing jobs, commute on packed trains, and argue about rent prices, even as sky-beams periodically erupt and costume brawls close down entire blocks. Such is life.

The Tier System

Power in this world isn't just felt. It's quantified, debated, and obsessed over by official organizations and armchair fandoms. The tier classification system ranks supers from F to S based on demonstrated threat scale.

F-Tier: Street-level threats affecting neighborhoods and small areas. Can win bar fights but struggle against coordinated opposition.

D-Tier: Large city block-wide impact. Capable of disrupting organized crime operations or causing significant property damage in localized areas.

C-Tier: District-level problems requiring coordinated response. Can hold entire city blocks and demand professional hero intervention.

B-Tier: Reliable city-level threats that draw major media attention. Actively recruited and their actions reshape local politics.

A-Tier: National-level dangers requiring international effort. Face heavy regulation, registration requirements, and constant scrutiny.

S-Tier: Global threats that reshape geopolitics. Their existence forces treaties, protocols, and entire defense strategies.

Tier lists are published by multiple entities, each with different methodologies and biases. Online communities endlessly debate rankings, creating sub-tiers and custom classifications. Your tier affe
